title: Typography
intro: Typography in the wireframe system is two layers: a token file that defines the type scale, weights, and line heights as `--wf-*` variables, and a flat set of `wf-text-*` classes you compose by hand. Every text element starts with the base `.wf-text` class and adds a size, then optional weight and color modifiers.

blocks.body:
The contract is `.wf-text` first, then one size class. `.wf-text` sets the system font stack, `line-height: 1.5`, zeroes the margin, and paints `#1a1a2e` — so a bare `

` already reads correctly. Layer a size on top: `wf-text-h1` through `wf-text-h4` for headings, `wf-text-body` for paragraphs, `wf-text-label` / `wf-text-helper` / `wf-text-caption` for the small print. Weight and color are separate modifier axes. Headings and labels carry their own weight, but body text starts at `400` and you promote it with `wf-text-medium`, `wf-text-semibold`, or `wf-text-bold`. Color modifiers (`wf-text-secondary`, `wf-text-error`, and friends) override the base `#1a1a2e` — they win because they come later in the stylesheet. The `--wf-*` tokens in `tokens/typography.css` are the design source of truth: a 7-level scale from `--wf-text-xs` (11px) to `--wf-text-2xl` (32px), four weights, three line heights. The `wf-text-*` classes are a curated subset wired with literal px values rather than `var()` references — so the tokens document the system and the classes are what you actually type.

blocks.title: Headings — the wf-text-h* scale
blocks.caption: .wf-text-h1 Page Title (24px), .wf-text-h2 Section Header (18px), .wf-text-h3 Subsection (16px), .wf-text-h4 Card Title (14px) — all semibold (600), all tightened by line-height and the h1/h2 negative letter-spacing.

blocks.title: Type scale tokens
blocks.intro: The 7-level scale from tokens/typography.css. The wf-text-* classes lock in a subset of these as literal px — the tokens are the full vocabulary and the comment on each documents its intended home.

rows.use: Fine print, badges, timestamps — the size behind .wf-text-caption.
Captions, helper text, table cells — the size behind .wf-text-label and .wf-text-helper.
Body text, default size — the compact default behind .wf-text-body and .wf-text-h4.
Emphasized body, large inputs — the size behind .wf-text-h3.
Section headings, card titles — the size behind .wf-text-h2.
Page headings, modal titles — the size behind .wf-text-h1.
Hero text, major headings — defined for reach; no default class ships at this size.

blocks.title: Weight, line-height & letter-spacing tokens
blocks.intro: The supporting axes. Weights map one-to-one onto the wf-text-{normal,medium,semibold,bold} modifiers; the line-height and tracking tokens are baked into the size classes.

rows.use: The system font stack — native look, zero web-font load. Mirrored literally in .wf-text.
Monospace for code, data, and technical content.
Regular body text — .wf-text-normal and the resting weight of .wf-text-body.
Subtle emphasis, labels — .wf-text-medium and the weight inside .wf-text-label.
Strong emphasis, buttons, headings — .wf-text-semibold and the weight of every wf-text-h*.
Body text default — the line-height baked into .wf-text and .wf-text-body.
All caps, labels — the spacing intent behind .wf-text-label's uppercase treatment.

blocks.title: Size classes
blocks.intro: Pick exactly one and pair it with .wf-text. Headings carry their own 600 weight and tightened line-height; body and small-print sizes carry their own color where it differs from the base.

rows.name: wf-text-h1
rows.when: Page titles and modal titles. 24px / 600 / line-height 1.3, letter-spacing -0.02em.

rows.name: wf-text-h2
rows.when: Section headers. 18px / 600 / line-height 1.4, letter-spacing -0.01em.

rows.name: wf-text-h3
rows.when: Subsections. 16px / 600 / line-height 1.4.

rows.name: wf-text-h4
rows.when: Card titles and small headers. 14px / 600 / line-height 1.4.

rows.name: wf-text-body
rows.when: Default paragraph and content text. 14px / 400 / line-height 1.5.
rows.code: Regular body text for paragraphs and content.

rows.name: wf-text-label
rows.when: Form labels and data labels. 12px / 500 / uppercase, letter-spacing 0.05em, color #6b7280.

rows.name: wf-text-helper
rows.when: Form hints and descriptions under an input. 12px / 400 / color #6b7280.
rows.code: We'll never share your email.

rows.name: wf-text-caption
rows.when: Small annotations and timestamps. 11px / 400 / color #9ca3af.

blocks.title: Body text and weight modifiers
blocks.caption: .wf-text-body resting at 14px / 400, then promoted with .wf-text-medium (500) and .wf-text-semibold (600). Weight is a separate axis layered on top of the size class.

blocks.title: Weight & color modifiers
blocks.intro: Optional axes layered after the size class. Stack one weight and one color as needed — they override the base because they come later in the stylesheet.

rows.name: wf-text-normal
rows.when: Force 400 — the resting weight, rarely needed explicitly.

rows.name: wf-text-medium
rows.when: Promote body to 500 for subtle emphasis.

rows.name: wf-text-semibold
rows.when: Promote to 600 — emphasized values, inline strong text.

rows.name: wf-text-bold
rows.when: Promote to 700 — maximum emphasis, critical text.

rows.name: wf-text-secondary
rows.when: Muted gray (#6b7280) for supporting copy.
rows.code: Manage and track all your active contracts

rows.name: wf-text-error
rows.when: Negative states (#dc2626) — validation messages, error copy.
rows.code: Please enter a valid email address

rows.name: wf-text-link
rows.when: Clickable text (#2563eb) — underlines and darkens to #1d4ed8 on hover.

rows.name: wf-text-inverse
rows.when: White (#ffffff) for text on dark backgrounds.
rows.code: Inverse text on dark background

blocks.title: Labels and helper text
blocks.caption: .wf-text-label (12px uppercase, wide tracking, #6b7280) for form labels and .wf-text-helper (12px, muted #6b7280) for hints — the small print of a form field.

blocks.title: Label component — vertical (stacked)
blocks.caption: The .wf-label-group pairing: an uppercase .wf-text-label stacked above its value with a 4px gap. The default narrative-first form posture; horizontal and spread are the sibling layouts.

blocks.title: Label group layouts
blocks.intro: Key-value pairs use .wf-label-group as the wrapper, then a layout modifier changes the axis. Stacked is the default; add a modifier for inline or justified.

rows.name: wf-label-group
rows.when: Default stacked pairing — label above value, column flex, 4px gap.
rows.code: Contract ID CNT-2024-001

rows.name: wf-label-horizontal
rows.when: Inline label + value on one row — switches to row flex, 8px gap, label gets a 100px min-width.
rows.code: Status: Active

rows.name: wf-label-spread
rows.when: Label left, value right — row flex with justify-content: space-between. Totals and summary rows.
rows.code: Total Amount $12,500.00

rows.name: wf-label-colon
rows.when: Appends a ':' to the label via ::after — for inline label styling without typing the colon.
rows.code: Status Active

blocks.title: Composing a form field
blocks.code: We'll never share your email. Please enter a valid email address
blocks.note: Add .wf-required to a label and a red asterisk is appended via ::after — no markup change needed.

blocks.title: A detail card header and value grid
blocks.code:
Order Details

Confirmed
Order Number ORD-2024-78542
Customer John Smith
blocks.note: wf-section-header pairs a heading with a trailing action or status and draws a bottom border; add wf-section-header-simple to drop the border.

items.rule: Always start with .wf-text, then add exactly one size class.
items.why: .wf-text carries the font stack, line-height, margin reset, and base color — without it a size class inherits whatever the surrounding element happens to be.
The system defaults compact: .wf-text-body and .wf-text-h4 both sit at 14px. Reach for .wf-text-md (16px) deliberately when you want emphasized body, not by accident.

items.rule: Promote weight with a modifier; don't switch to a heading class for emphasis.
items.why: A heading class also changes size and line-height. To bold a value inside a sentence, add .wf-text-semibold — the size stays put.

items.rule: Use .wf-text-label for form and data labels, .wf-text-helper for hints, .wf-text-caption for timestamps.
items.why: They share the 12px/11px small-print band but encode different intent — label is uppercase 500, helper is muted 400, caption is the faintest 11px #9ca3af.

items.rule: Wrap key-value pairs in .wf-label-group and let a layout modifier set the axis.
items.why: Stacked, horizontal, and spread all share the same wrapper — switching layout is a one-class change, not a markup rewrite.
The size classes set only size/weight/line-height — they rely on .wf-text for the font stack, margin reset, and color. Omit it and you inherit the page's defaults.

items.dont: <span class="wf-text wf-text-h4"> just to bold it
items.why: wf-text-h4 is 14px/600 too, but it's semantically a heading — using it for inline emphasis muddies the document outline and the wireframe's intent.
wf-text-label already encodes the 12px size, 500 weight, muted color, and wide tracking that defines a label — rebuilding it from body + transform drifts off-spec.
.wf-required appends the red asterisk via ::after — typing it manually loses the consistent #dc2626 color and 2px offset.

items.title: The wf-text-* classes use literal px, not the tokens
items.detail: tokens/typography.css defines --wf-text-base etc., but components/typography/typography.css hardcodes font-size: 14px instead of var(--wf-text-base). Edit a token and the wf-text-* classes won't move — change both, or treat the tokens as documentation of the scale the classes already follow.

items.title: Headings ship their own weight and line-height
items.detail: All four wf-text-h* are 600 with line-height 1.3–1.4, and h1/h2 add negative letter-spacing. They override .wf-text's 1.5 line-height — so a heading is more than just a bigger size, and stacking a weight modifier on it is redundant.

items.title: .wf-text-label and .wf-text-helper carry color, not just size
items.detail: Both paint #6b7280 (gray) out of the box, and .wf-text-caption paints #9ca3af. If you need them dark, you'll fight the class — they're built for muted small print, which is usually what you want.

items.title: Headings shrink at the 640px breakpoint
items.detail: A max-width: 640px media query drops h1 to 20px, h2 to 16px, h3 to 14px, and collapses wf-label-horizontal into a stacked column. Wireframes captured wide will read smaller on narrow viewports — expected, but worth knowing before you screenshot.

items.title: wf-label-horizontal pins a 100px label column
items.detail: The label gets min-width: 100px and flex-shrink: 0, so short labels still reserve the full column. Below 640px that min-width is removed and the layout stacks — so inline labels are a desktop affordance.
